title: Hypenia irregularis
taxonomy: Hypenia irregularis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions of South America. It belongs to the genus Hypenia and is further classified as a member of the subfamily Lamioideae. The species name 'irregularis' refers to the unique morphology of the plant's flowers. Hypenia irregularis is a relatively rare and understudied species within the Lamiaceae family.

intro: Hypenia irregularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the Brazilian sage. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Hypenia irregularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
